title: Cotoneaster fulvidus
intro: Cotoneaster fulvidus is a small deciduous shrub native to China, known for its medicinal properties and ornamental value. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cotoneaster fulvidus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cotoneaster fulvidus is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and tablets.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster fulvidus is classified in the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes a wide range of plants, from shrubs and trees to vines and herbs. Within the family Rosaceae, Cotoneaster fulvidus is placed in the genus Cotoneaster, which consists of about 10-15 species of flowering shrubs native to Asia and Europe. The genus Cotoneaster is further divided into several sections, with Cotoneaster fulvidus typically classified within the section Cotoneaster sect. Cotoneaster. The specific classification of Cotoneaster fulvidus reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within the family Rosaceae.
